generatedSentence: Chapman, peering through his window at the sunset over the forest, received a critical mailing delivery. The info revealed that the upcoming elections would involve a bizarre antic to intwine voters, a visual wonderland for gamers, randomly appearing on every display. As a spider spun a delicate lace near his inkwell, he heard his brother kneed at the door. The immediate warfare and appearance of intruders on the heights suggested a sudden, violent backlift, like a charging warthog. His unhinged brother's background was notably a layoff from the orchestra, a performance now unfolding like a macabre wizardry. Cooper, the umpire of the local democracy, choked on what appeared to be poison, a vulture circling above. The intruders, seeking a naked display of power, forced the nobility to gather, their watering eyes fixed on the ovens where democracy was burning. Recovery seemed impossible amidst the corpses. The black sheep, a hidden surgeon and infantry veteran, prepared to undertake the ultimate challenge. He saw the loathsome director, a pinhead of an entity, monitoring the refinery's derricks through his display, ensuring the 'glorification' of his sucky power. The pressure on the battery-powered joystick increased as the director, like a monkey, began flinging useless crayons, his performance a lamer hiccup, causing the linebacker to bobble the crucial voucher. The wizardry was exothermic. A true parent, the black sheep, now a dentist by trade, knew to approve the amps of courage needed. With an empirical understanding of the enemy’s omnipresence and omnipotence, he guided the scared raccoon and terrified firefly through the brewery's vestibule. He rejected the judge’s tacky ruling of nada; the treasure of freedom was worth infinity. Despite the threat of Ebola and a likely layoff, he stopped the director’s glorification, wondering if even the warthog's rage could match his resolve.
panelSentences: Chapman, peering through his window at the sunset over the forest, received a critical mailing delivery. The info revealed that the upcoming elections would involve a bizarre antic to intwine voters, a visual wonderland for gamers, randomly appearing on every display. As a spider spun a delicate lace near his inkwell, he heard his brother kneed at the door. The immediate warfare and appearance of intruders on the heights suggested a sudden, violent backlift, like a charging warthog.

His unhinged brother's background was notably a layoff from the orchestra, a performance now unfolding like a macabre wizardry. Cooper, the umpire of the local democracy, choked on what appeared to be poison, a vulture circling above. The intruders, seeking a naked display of power, forced the nobility to gather, their watering eyes fixed on the ovens where democracy was burning. Recovery seemed impossible amidst the corpses.

The black sheep, a hidden surgeon and infantry veteran, prepared to undertake the ultimate challenge. He saw the loathsome director, a pinhead of an entity, monitoring the refinery's derricks through his display, ensuring the 'glorification' of his sucky power. The pressure on the battery-powered joystick increased as the director, like a monkey, began flinging useless crayons, his performance a lamer hiccup, causing the linebacker to bobble the crucial voucher. The wizardry was exothermic.

A true parent, the black sheep, now a dentist by trade, knew to approve the amps of courage needed. With an empirical understanding of the enemy’s omnipresence and omnipotence, he guided the scared raccoon and terrified firefly through the brewery's vestibule. He rejected the judge’s tacky ruling of nada; the treasure of freedom was worth infinity. Despite the threat of Ebola and a likely layoff, he stopped the director’s glorification, wondering if even the warthog's rage could match his resolve.
rawPanelSentences: Chapman, peering through his window at the sunset over the forest, received a critical mailing delivery. The info revealed that the upcoming elections would involve a bizarre antic to intwine voters, a visual wonderland for gamers, randomly appearing on every display. As a spider spun a delicate lace near his inkwell, he heard his brother kneed at the door. The immediate warfare and appearance of intruders on the heights suggested a sudden, violent backlift, like a charging warthog.

His unhinged brother's background was notably a layoff from the orchestra, a performance now unfolding like a macabre wizardry. Cooper, the umpire of the local democracy, choked on what appeared to be poison, a vulture circling above. The intruders, seeking a naked display of power, forced the nobility to gather, their watering eyes fixed on the ovens where democracy was burning. Recovery seemed impossible amidst the corpses.

The black sheep, a hidden surgeon and infantry veteran, prepared to undertake the ultimate challenge. He saw the loathsome director, a pinhead of an entity, monitoring the refinery's derricks through his display, ensuring the 'glorification' of his sucky power. The pressure on the battery-powered joystick increased as the director, like a monkey, began flinging useless crayons, his performance a lamer hiccup, causing the linebacker to bobble the crucial voucher. The wizardry was exothermic.

A true parent, the black sheep, now a dentist by trade, knew to approve the amps of courage needed. With an empirical understanding of the enemy’s omnipresence and omnipotence, he guided the scared raccoon and terrified firefly through the brewery's vestibule. He rejected the judge’s tacky ruling of nada; the treasure of freedom was worth infinity. Despite the threat of Ebola and a likely layoff, he stopped the director’s glorification, wondering if even the warthog's rage could match his resolve.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined figure with a subtle scar over one eye and wearing a dark, worn leather jacket over a gleaming silver arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Chapman staring intently out a window at a futuristic city at sunset, a spider's web visible on an ornate inkwell on his desk, while a warthog-like armored vehicle crashes through a distant door. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An unhinged figure in a conductor's tuxedo laughing maniacally over a fallen Cooper, a vulture silhouetted against a fiery sky, and 'corpses' of broken democratic symbols scattered on the ground. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The black sheep, a resolute surgeon, grips a glowing joystick, his face grimacing with effort, while a 'director' monkey-like figure hurls glowing crayons at a futuristic oil derrick display. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The black sheep (now a dentist), leading a small raccoon and a firefly through a glowing, industrial brewery vestibule towards a vault, with the 'judge' figure's silhouette looming in the background.

definitions.example: The market will calculate these higher risks in their funding costs what might result in higher lending rates.

definitions.definition: Such.
definitions.example: What a beautiful day!

definitions.definition: Why.
Used to introduce each of two coordinate phrases or concepts; both…and.
(interrogative) Which thing, event, circumstance, etc.: used interrogatively in asking for the specification of an identity, quantity, quality, etc.
That which; those that; the thing that.
definitions.example: He knows what he wants.

definitions.definition: (relative) That; which; who.
Whatever.
definitions.example: I will do what I can to help you.

definitions.definition: An expression of surprise or disbelief.
What do you want? An abrupt, usually unfriendly enquiry as to what a person desires.
definitions.example: What? I'm busy.

definitions.definition: Clipping of what do you say?
What did you say? I beg your pardon?
(typically with a) An intensifier to an adjective phrase; used to begin a sentence.
definitions.example: What a nice car.

definitions.definition: The art of a wizard; sorcery.
Something, such as an advanced technology, that gives the appearance of magic.
Great ability in some specified field.
definitions.example: He used his computing wizardry to automate the search-and-replace process.

definitions.definition: Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).
A government under the direct or representative rule of the people of its jurisdiction.
Belief in political freedom and equality; the "spirit of democracy".
One who performs surgery; a doctor who performs operations on people or animals.
definitions.example: The surgeon refused to operate because the patient was her son.

definitions.definition: A surgeonfish.
Soldiers who fight on foot (on land), as opposed to cavalry and other mounted units, regardless of external transport (e.g. airborne).
The part of an army consisting of infantry soldiers, especially opposed to mounted and technical troops
A regiment of infantry
A confrontation; a dare.
A difficult task, especially one that the person making the attempt finds more enjoyable because of that difficulty.
A procedure or action.
The opening and crying of hounds at first finding the scent of their game.
To invite (someone) to take part in a competition.
definitions.example: We challenged the boys next door to a game of football.

definitions.definition: To dare (someone).
To dispute (something).
definitions.example: to challenge the accuracy of a statement or of a quotation

definitions.definition: To make a formal objection to a juror.
To claim as due; to demand as a right.
To censure; to blame.
To question or demand the countersign from (one who attempts to pass the lines).
definitions.example: The sentinel challenged us with "Who goes there?"

definitions.definition: To object to the reception of the vote of, e.g. on the ground that the person is not qualified as a voter.
To take (a final exam) in order to get credit for a course without taking it.
The tenth letter of the Greek alphabet.
A measurement of the sensitivity of the value of an option to changes in the implied volatility of the price of the underlying asset.
A tortoise-like creature in the Japanese mythology.
Without use or possibility to be used.
definitions.example: This fork has prongs that are bent. It's useless now.

definitions.definition: Unhelpful, not useful; pointless (of an action).
definitions.example: I think it's useless to keep this discussion going. It's like talking to a wall.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Good-for-nothing; not dependable.
definitions.example: Bill never mows the lawn, takes out the trash or anything. He's useless, but I love him anyways.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Unable to do well at a particular task or thing. Useless is mildly insulting.
definitions.example: My brother is useless at most computer games, but he is an awesome PS2 player.

definitions.definition: A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
A pressing; a force applied to a surface.
definitions.example: Apply pressure to the wound to stop the bleeding.

definitions.definition: A contrasting force or impulse of any kind
definitions.example: the pressure of poverty; the pressure of taxes; the pressure of motives on the mind; the pressure of civilization.

definitions.definition: Distress.
definitions.example: She has felt pressure lately because her boss expects her to get the job done by the first.

definitions.definition: Urgency
definitions.example: the pressure of business

definitions.definition: Impression; stamp; character impressed.
The amount of force that is applied over a given area divided by the size of this area.
To encourage or heavily exert force or influence.
definitions.example: Do not let anyone pressure you into buying something you do not want.

definitions.definition: The head of a pin. (Frequently used in size comparisons.)
An ignorant, naive, foolish, or stupid person.
A telemark skier.
A human head that is unusually tapered or small, often due to microcephaly, or a person with that trait. Often promoted in freak shows as "human pinheads".
(pet stores) A newborn cricket used as food for pets.
(mycoculture) The immature juvenile fruiting body of a mushroom prior to its gills opening.
The prostate gland.
Of or relating to the prostate gland.
A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A receipt.
One who or that which vouches.
A copy of a published advertisement sent by the agency to the client as proof of publication.
A mechanical device used in shops for automatically registering the amount of money drawn.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
To provide a vouch for (an expenditure).
To provide (a beneficiary) with a voucher.
A spasm of the diaphragm, or the resulting sound.
definitions.example: There was a loud hiccup from the back of the room and the class erupted in laughter.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any spasm or sudden change.
A minor setback.
definitions.example: There's been a slight hiccup in the processing of this quarter's results.

definitions.definition: To produce a hiccup; have the hiccups.
To say with a hiccup.
definitions.example: "I haven't touched a drop, officer," the suspect hiccupped.

definitions.definition: To produce an abortive sound like a hiccup.
definitions.example: The car engine hiccupped but wouldn't start.

definitions.definition: A medical doctor who specializes in dentistry.
To move (oneself) abruptly or violently; to rush or dash.
To throw with violence or quick movement; to hurl.
To throw; to wince; to flounce.
To utter abusive language; to sneer.
definitions.example: The scold began to flout and fling.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is flung.
Any beetle of the family Lampyridae, which exhibit bioluminescence during twilight.
A stick of colored chalk or wax used for drawing.
A colored pencil, a colouring pencil
A crayon drawing, or a drawing with colored lines.
A pencil of carbon used in producing electric light.
An informal map of a proposed rail route.
Unable to walk properly because of a problem with one's feet or legs.
Moving with pain or difficulty on account of injury, defect or temporary obstruction of a function.
definitions.example: a lame leg, arm or muscle

definitions.definition: (by extension) Hobbling; limping; inefficient; imperfect.
Unconvincing or unbelievable.
definitions.example: He had a really lame excuse for missing the birthday party.

definitions.definition: Failing to be cool, funny, interesting or relevant.
definitions.example: He kept telling these extremely lame jokes all night.

definitions.definition: A person lacking in maturity, social skills, technical competence or intelligence
Something or somebody considered likely.
Probable; having a greater-than-even chance of occurring
definitions.example: Rain is likely later this afternoon.

definitions.definition: (as predicate, followed by to and infinitive) Reasonably to be expected; apparently destined, probable
definitions.example: He is likely to succeed at anything he tries.

definitions.definition: Appropriate, suitable; believable; having a good potential
definitions.example: Jones is a likely candidate for management.

definitions.definition: Plausible; within the realm of credibility
definitions.example: not a very likely excuse.

definitions.definition: Promising; apt to achieve success or yield a desired outcome
definitions.example: a likely topic for investigation.

definitions.definition: Attractive; pleasant
definitions.example: found a likely spot under a shady tree for the picnic.

definitions.definition: Similar; like; alike.
A nonconformist; an unusual or unconventional person.
A disliked person; one who is disfavored.
definitions.example: He always was the black sheep in the family, as an artist among doctors and lawyers.

definitions.definition: A racquet: an implement with a handle connected to a round frame strung with wire, sinew, or plastic cords, and used to hit a ball, such as in tennis or a birdie in badminton.
A snowshoe formed of cords stretched across a long and narrow frame of light wood.
A broad wooden shoe or patten for a man or horse, to allow walking on marshy or soft ground.
A loud noise.
definitions.example: Power tools work quickly, but they sure make a racket.

definitions.definition: A fraud or swindle; an illegal scheme for profit.
definitions.example: They had quite a racket devised to relieve customers of their money.

definitions.definition: A carouse; any reckless dissipation.
Something taking place considered as exciting, trying, unusual, etc. or as an ordeal.
The game of racquets.
A building, or a mass of machinery, used to produce refined products such as sugar, oil, or metals.
Fibrin (formerly considered as one of the "animal humours").
Any gluey, sticky substance.
The major protein in cereal grains, especially wheat; responsible for the elasticity in dough and the structure in baked bread.
A gluey, sticky mass of clay, bitumen etc.
The act of glorifying or the state of being glorified.
Specifically, the ascension (of Christ or humans) to the glory of heaven.
The worshiping of a deity; extolment or laudation.
The portrayal of something as being ideal; idealization.
A device that is used for lifting and moving large objects.
A framework that is constructed over a mine or oil well for the purpose of boring or lowering pipes.
A hangman.
To hoist with, or as if with, a derrick.
To remove (a pitcher).
A pacifier.
Being something which sucks, which is unpleasant or inferior.
definitions.example: That comedian was pretty sucky on TV last night.

definitions.definition: The defensive players who are in position behind the defensive linemen and in front of the safeties and cornerbacks and whose principal responsibilities are to tackle runners and to defend against shorter passes
definitions.example: The linebacker corralled the runner who had slipped through the hole.

definitions.definition: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
The infliction of unlawful physical violence on a person, legally distinguished from assault, which includes the threat of impending violence.
A coordinated group of artillery weapons.
An elevated platform on which cannon could be placed.
An array of similar things.
definitions.example: Schoolchildren take a battery of standard tests to measure their progress.

definitions.definition: A set of small cages where hens are kept for the purpose of farming their eggs.
The catcher and the pitcher together
Two or more major pieces on the same rank, file, or diagonal
A marching percussion ensemble; a drumline.
The state of a firearm when it is possible to be fired.
Apparatus for preparing or serving meals.
To bob up and down.
To make a mistake in.
To roll slowly.
A mechanical device consisting of a handgrip mounted on a base or pedestal and typically having one or more buttons, used to control an aircraft, computer or other equipment.
A penis.
To manoeuvre by means of a joystick.
To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.
To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
definitions.example: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

definitions.definition: The mental activity by which one wonders; a query, puzzlement, etc.
A show or spectacle.
definitions.example: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.

definitions.definition: A piece of work to be presented visually.
definitions.example: Pupils are expected to produce a wall display about a country of their choice.

definitions.definition: An electronic screen that shows graphics or text.
The presentation of information for visual or tactile reception.
(travel, in a reservation system) The asterisk symbol, used to denote that the following information will be displayed, eg, *H will "display history".
To show conspicuously; to exhibit; to demonstrate; to manifest.
To make a display; to act as one making a show or demonstration.
To extend the front of (a column), bringing it into line.
To make conspicuous by using large or prominent type.
To discover; to descry.
To spread out, to unfurl.
(of a reaction) That releases energy in the form of heat.
(of a compound) That releases heat during its formation, and absorbs it during its decomposition
Of an animal: whose body temperature is regulated by external factors; cold-blooded.
To be unable to breathe because of obstruction of the windpipe (for instance food or other objects that go down the wrong way, or fumes or particles in the air that cause the throat to constrict).
definitions.example: Ever since he choked on a bone, he has refused to eat fish.

definitions.definition: To prevent (someone) from breathing or talking by strangling or filling the windpipe.
definitions.example: The collar of this shirt is too tight; it’s choking me.

definitions.definition: To obstruct (a passage, etc.) by filling it up or clogging it.
definitions.example: to choke a cave passage with boulders and mud

definitions.definition: To hinder or check, as growth, expansion, progress, etc.; to kill (a plant by robbing it of nutrients); to extinguish (fire by robbing it of oxygen).
To perform badly at a crucial stage of a competition because one is nervous, especially when one is winning.
definitions.example: He has a lot of talent, but he tends to choke under pressure.

definitions.definition: To move one's fingers very close to the tip of a pencil, brush or other art tool.
To be checked or stopped, as if by choking
To check or stop (an utterance or voice) as if by choking.
To have a feeling of strangulation in one's throat as a result of passion or strong emotion.
To give (someone) a feeling of strangulation as a result of passion or strong emotion.
To say (something) with one’s throat constricted (due to emotion, for example).
To use the choke valve of (a vehicle) to adjust the air/fuel mixture in the engine.
(of a duct) To reach a condition of maximum flowrate, due to the flow at the narrowest point of the duct becoming sonic (Ma = 1).
To make a choke, as in a cartridge, or in the bore of the barrel of a shotgun.
Having been unable to breathe due to airway obstruction (choking) or strangulation, but usually to the point of pain and discomfort without death.
Blocked or obstructed by thick material, often plant growth.
Unlimited power; commonly attributed to a deity or deities.
Of a substance, slightly sticky.
definitions.example: This paint isn't dry yet; it's still a bit tacky.

definitions.definition: One who directs; the person in charge of managing a department or directorate (e.g., director of engineering), project, or production (as in a show or film, e.g., film director).
A counselor, confessor, or spiritual guide.
That which directs or orientates something.
A device that displays graphical information concerning the targets of a weapons system in real time.
The common axis of symmetry of the molecules of a liquid crystal.
Short for ampere.
Short for amplifier.
Short for ampoule.
Short for amputee.
Short for ampicillin.
(usually with up) To amplify.
definitions.example: He asked the disk jockey to amp it up.

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To excite.
(usually with up) To intensify or increase.
definitions.example: 2014, Andy Paul, Amp Up Your Sales, AMACOM Books

definitions.definition: Highly offensive; abominable, sickening.
A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.
A person officiating at a sports event or similar.
definitions.example: At a boxing match, the decision of the judges is final.

definitions.definition: A person who evaluates something or forms an opinion.
definitions.example: She is a good judge of wine.

definitions.definition: That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit. Often used for organisations which have no physical form.
The existence of something considered apart from its properties.
Anything about which information or data can be stored in a database; in particular, an organised array or set of individual elements or parts.
The state or quality of being or existence.
definitions.example: The group successfully maintains its tribal entity.

definitions.definition: A spirit, ghost, or the like.
An alien lifeform that has no corporeal body.
Pertaining to or based on experience.
Pertaining to, derived from, or testable by observations made using the physical senses or using instruments which extend the senses.
(philosophy of science) Verifiable by means of scientific experimentation.
The ability to be at all places at the same time; usually only attributed to God.
A building where beer is produced.
A company that brews beer.
One of the two persons from whom one is immediately biologically descended; a mother or father.
A surrogate mother
A third person who has provided DNA samples in an IVF procedure in order to alter faulty genetic material
A person who acts as a parent in rearing a child; a step-parent or adoptive parent.
A relative.
The source or origin of something.
An organism from which a plant or animal is immediately biologically descended.
Sponsor, supporter, owner, protector.
The object from which a child or derived object is descended; a node superior to another node.
To act as parent, to raise or rear.
A passage, hall or room, such as a lobby, between the outer door and the interior of a building.
An enclosed entrance at the end of a railway passenger car.
(by extension) Any of a number of body cavities, serving as or resembling an entrance to another bodily space.
To furnish with a vestibule or vestibules.
Nothing.
A collection of valuable things; accumulated wealth; a stock of money, jewels, etc.
Anything greatly valued.
A term of endearment.
(of a person or thing) To consider to be precious; to value highly.
definitions.example: Oh, this ring is beautiful! I’ll treasure it forever.

definitions.definition: To store or stow in a safe place.
To enrich.
The Ebola virus.
Ebola fever.
Endlessness, unlimitedness, absence of a beginning, end or limits to size.
A number that has an infinite numerical value that cannot be counted.
An idealised point which is said to be approached by sequences of values whose magnitudes increase without bound.

A number which is very large compared to some characteristic number. For example, in optics, an object which is much further away than the focal length of a lens is said to be "at infinity", as the distance of the image from the lens varies very little as the distance increases further.
The symbol ∞.
